Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
**Garland** in JJS0 is a ceremony operation that retires a heat in flight and spins up a fresh continuation, preserving the source for retrospective.

Mechanics (`JJSCGL-garland.adoc`):
- Source heat gets a `garlanded-` prefix + zero-padded sequence suffix on its silks (e.g., `garlanded-jjk-post-alpha-polish-01`), is furloughed to `stabled`, and gets a steeplechase marker like `Garlanded at pace {N} — magnificent service`.
- A new continuation heat is nominated with the next sequence (`jjk-post-alpha-polish-02`), starts `racing`, copies the source paddock, and is moved to position 0 of the order.
- Paces partition: actionable (`rough` or `bridled`) transfer to the continuation; `complete` and `abandoned` stay with the garlanded source.
- Takes a single arg (firemark of the heat to garland); returns a JSON summary with old/new firemarks, old/new silks, and counts transferred/retained.

In short: it's the "honor the long-running heat and start fresh" operation — distinct from `retire` (archive a finished heat) and `transfer` (move specific paces between existing heats).

content: You asked what "garland" means in JJS0; I explained it's a ceremony that furloughs a heat with a `garlanded-NN` silks prefix and spins up a fresh continuation heat carrying the actionable paces. No next action pending. (disable recaps in /config)

## Assessment: Eliminate `garland`

### Files to delete (3, ~709 lines)
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrgl_garland.rs` — 144 lines, MCP wrapper
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjtgl_garland.rs` — 422 lines, integration test fixture
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCGL-garland.adoc` — 143 lines, command spec

### Major changes (1 file)
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjro_ops.rs` — drop `jjrg_garland` (~140 lines, lines ~834–980). The function is self-contained — no other ops call it.

### Minor changes (single-line / few-line edits)
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/lib.rs` — remove `pub mod jjrgl_garland;` and `mod jjtgl_garland;`
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrm_mcp.rs` — remove use-import + dispatch arm (2 sites)
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrg_gallops.rs` — drop `jjrg_garland` from re-export and method shim (3 lines)
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrt_types.rs` + `jjrt_v3_types.rs` — drop `jjrg_GarlandArgs` / `jjrg_GarlandResult` structs (~10 lines each)
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrq_query.rs` — drop `jjrq_build_garlanded_silks` + 2 unit tests (~20 lines). Verify `jjrq_parse_silks_sequence` and `jjrq_build_continuation_silks` aren't used elsewhere; if garland-only, drop them too.
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrn_notch.rs` — remove `Garland` variant from `jjrn_HeatAction` enum + 2 match arms
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrnm_markers.rs` — remove `JJRNM_GARLAND` constant + table entry
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJS0_JobJockeySpec.adoc` — remove the `include::JJSCGL-garland.adoc[]` line
- `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCRS-restring.adoc` — strike "garlanding," from one prose sentence

### Out of scope (do not touch)
- `.claude/jjm/retired/jjh_b260124-r260124-jjk-garland-feature.md` and other retired heat archives — historical record
- `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json` and `.claude/jjm/jjp_uAl5.md` — live state will naturally drift; the steeplechase marker text "Garlanded at pace 5" is just a string in a paddock file, harmless to leave
- The verb table in CLAUDE.md / verb dispatch — `garland` already isn't a quick verb there (per the V4 work in flight visible in `jjg_gallops.json`, it's planned for elimination from the upper API as well)

### Shape
About 700 lines deleted, ~30 lines edited across 11 source/spec files. Self-contained — `jjrg_garland` has no callers in `jjro_ops.rs` and the steeplechase marker constant `JJRNM_GARLAND` is only referenced by the action enum that maps to it. No data migration needed (the gallops.json silks string `garlanded-rbk-mvp-3-public-docs-refresh-01` is just a label; the heat is already stabled).

Risk: low. Mostly mechanical deletion. Main judgment call is whether `jjrq_parse_silks_sequence` / `jjrq_build_continuation_silks` have other consumers — quick grep at execution time will tell.

Mechanical deletion sweep — the `garland` verb is going away. Self-contained: no callers outside its own module and type pair. ~700 lines deleted, ~30 lines edited.

- No `garland`/`Garland`/`GARLAND` tokens remain under `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/` source or specs
- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` clean, `tt/vow-t.Test.sh` green

- CLAUDE.md verb table — garland is already absent there

Launch two agents in a single message. They operate on disjoint file sets, so wall-clock time is bounded by Agent A (Rust build).

In `jjrq_query.rs`, drop `jjrq_build_garlanded_silks` and its tests; if `jjrq_parse_silks_sequence` and `jjrq_build_continuation_silks` have no remaining callers (grep before dropping), excise them too. **Agent B — spec sweep.** Delete `JJSCGL-garland.adoc`. Drop the `include::JJSCGL-garland.adoc[]` line from `JJS0_JobJockeySpec.adoc`. Strike "garlanding," from the prose enumeration in `JJSCRS-restring.adoc`. No build gate on asciidoc.
